intro.heading: Clubs & Initiatives
intro.lead1: Our PTA is proud to support a range of enriching clubs and initiatives that help students explore their interests, build new skills, and grow in confidence beyond the classroom.
intro.lead2: These opportunities reflect our commitment to nurturing well-rounded learners and an inclusive, vibrant school community.
intro.contactNote: Want to get involved? Email us at jerseycityps5pta@gmail.com.
clubs.heading: Clubs
chess.heading: Chess Club
chess.faq[0].question: How do I join?
chess.faq[0].answer: Email the PTA at jerseycityps5pta@gmail.com and we'll get you connected.
chess.faq[1].question: When do we meet?
chess.faq[1].answer: Fridays at 5pm in the school garden (or Gia Gelato if the weather is bad). Goal is once a week.
chess.faq[2].question: What are the rules during a meetup?
chess.faq[2].answer: 1. Everyone plays. 2. If someone asks you to play them, try to say yes. 3. Set up the board after you finish a game. 4. Don't touch other people's pieces. 5. Be kind.
chess.faq[3].question: Can I drop off my kindergartener?
chess.faq[3].answer: No. Chess Club does not supervise young children independently — they need to be old enough to self-release from CASPER.
chess.faq[4].question: How can I help?
chess.faq[4].answer: Show up, teach kids to play, volunteer to bring snacks, help set up before 4pm, help clean up after. If you know a good indoor spot for winter, that would be great too.
chess.faq[5].question: Who's running this?
chess.faq[5].answer: PTA secretary Jon Huang. Reach the PTA at jerseycityps5pta@gmail.com with any questions.
culture.heading: Culture Club
culture.mission: To cultivate an environment where individuals are kind, compassionate, and understanding toward one another.
culture.project[1]: 2nd Graders & BBBS Mentoring Program — Rocks Projects
culture.project[3]: Champion Readers Program: classroom activities themed around Kindness, Friendship, and Empathy
culture.project[4]: Kindness Challenge, Pennies for Patients Coin Drive, UNICEF, Make-A-Wish, and St. Jude's Children's Hospital collections
garden.heading: Garden Club
garden.who: Middle school grades — no experience needed.
garden.body: Each week members plant and tend seasonal flowers, vegetables, and herbs; learn about transplanting and composting; do nature-inspired crafts; and explore NJ natives, urban tree canopy, pollinators, birds, and insects.
musicTheatre.heading: Music Theatre
musicTheatre.body: The Dr. Michael Conti PS5 Theater Club offers musical theater to students in grades 4–8, culminating in a live performance. Rehearsals led by Ms. Valentin engage students creatively through singing, acting, set design, and costume making.
financialLiteracy.heading: Financial Literacy Club
financialLiteracy.body: Details coming soon. For direct information, reach out to the PTA at jerseycityps5pta@gmail.com.
initiatives.heading: Initiatives
initiatives.lead: We support enrichment through art residencies, school assemblies, and programs that promote inclusion, advocacy, and a love of reading.
artResidencies.heading: Art Residencies
artResidencies.theatreBody: Theatre with Speranza Theatre Company (K & 1st): students work with Speranza right in their classrooms through theater games and creative writing, expressing their unique voices and building cultural exposure. Speranza is a nonprofit, women-led NJ Theater Alliance member. The PS5 PTA raises funds to support this program — email jerseycityps5pta@gmail.com to join the committee.
artResidencies.danceBody: Dance with Segunda Quimbamba Folkloric Center (Pre-K): Pre-K students learn the rhythms, movements, and cultural traditions of Afro-Puerto Rican dance and music, building coordination, confidence, and cultural awareness.
schoolAssemblies.heading: School Assemblies
schoolAssemblies.body: PS5 hosts a variety of engaging assemblies including the Planetarium, Liberty Science Center (LSC), Rutgers Science Bus, Lizard Guy and a magician, Sk8 4 Life (wellness and resilience through skateboarding and storytelling), and middle school assemblies on anti-racism and internet safety.
bookFair.heading: Book Fair
bookFair.body: The PS5 PTA partners with the school for the annual Book Fair, fostering a love of reading and raising classroom funds. Our Books for the Classroom initiative lets families contribute directly to classroom supplies, snacks, and special projects.
freeLibrary.heading: PS5 Little Free Library
freeLibrary.body: Located at the main entrance. Bring books for kids — borrow and give freely!
invitedSpeakers.heading: Invited Speakers
invitedSpeakers.body: The PS5 PTA brings in guest speakers — authors, scientists, artists, and community leaders — to connect classroom subjects to real-world applications and spark curiosity for middle school students.
parentWorkshops.heading: Parent Workshops
parentWorkshops.body: Workshops on internet safety, anti-racism in education, and neurodiversity — supporting an informed, inclusive, and compassionate school community.
advocacy.heading: Advocacy Committee
advocacy.body: We are looking for parents and teachers to become participants at the board and city level. If you are interested, please email jerseycityps5pta@gmail.com.
specialEd.heading: Special Education
specialEd.body: Volunteer coordinator needed! We are looking for parents who can research applicable grants, notify the school, and help teachers write and submit them. Email jerseycityps5pta@gmail.com if interested.
getInvolved.heading: Get Involved
getInvolved.body: Have ideas? We want to hear from you! Reach out at jerseycityps5pta@gmail.com.
